# ddff_feature_flag (Resource)

Manages a Datadog feature flag.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "ddff_feature_flag" "new_checkout" {
  key                 = "new_checkout_flow"
  name                = "New checkout flow"
  description         = "Enables the redesigned checkout flow."
  value_type          = "BOOLEAN"
  default_variant_key = "off"

  variants {
    key   = "on"
    name  = "On"
    value = "true"
  }

  variants {
    key   = "off"
    name  = "Off"
    value = "false"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `description` (String) Description shown in the UI.
- `key` (String) Stable unique key referenced from code. Immutable.
- `name` (String) Human-readable name shown in the UI.
- `value_type` (String) Type of values the variants resolve to. One of `BOOLEAN`, `INTEGER`, `NUMERIC`, `STRING`, `JSON`. Immutable.

### Optional

- `default_variant_key` (String) Variant key returned when no rule matches. Must match one of the declared variants.
- `json_schema` (String) JSON schema used to validate variant values when `value_type` is `JSON`. Compared by JSON semantic equality so server-side reformatting (whitespace, key order) is not treated as drift.
- `variants` (Block Set) Variants the flag can resolve to. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--variants))

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String)
- `id` (String) UUID assigned by Datadog.
- `updated_at` (String)

<a id="nestedblock--variants"></a>
### Nested Schema for `variants`

Required:

- `key` (String)
- `name` (String)
- `value` (String) Value of the variant encoded as a string (e.g. `"true"`, `"42"`, JSON literal).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Import an existing feature flag by its UUID. Replace the placeholder
# below with the actual feature flag ID from the Datadog UI or API.
terraform import ddff_feature_flag.new_checkout 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
```
